summary: Implemented the Stage 7D read-only worktree state snapshot layer for injected git command evidence, branch/base/head/dirty tracking, diff summaries, and changed-path validation.

tests_run.command: uv run --no-sync python -B -m pytest tests/test_worktree_state.py -q -p no:cacheprovider

tests_run.summary: 284 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

Worktree state inspection uses an inject.evidence: inspect_worktree_state requires a CommandRunner, builds tuple argv for read-only git rev-parse, status, and diff commands, and focused tests assert no worktrees directory is created.

Snapshots capture branch name, base comm.evidence: WorktreeStateSnapshot carries branch, base_commit, head_commit, dirty, changed_files, diff_summary, changed_path_violations, and GitCommandEvidence entries for every command.

Changed files from status and diff summa.evidence: The inspector combines porcelain status paths with diff --name-only output, then calls validate_changed_files and returns violations on the snapshot without mutating planning state.

Focused tests cover clean snapshots, dir.evidence: tests/test_worktree_state.py covers a clean branch/diff snapshot, dirty README.md violation reporting, worktree_state_failed command classification, and WorktreeArtifactError before runner invocation for outside paths.

risks: Stage 7D remains read-only and injected-runner-only; it does not create, remove, prune, or repair worktrees.
Real git worktree creation, orphan detection, and cleanup commands still need separate guarded slices.
Diff summaries currently use git diff --name-only between base and head commits; richer patch/stat summaries can be added later if review artifacts need them.
follow_up_tasks: Shape a Stage 7E slice for cleanup and orphan detection commands behind validate_cleanup_target.
Wire WorktreeStateSnapshot evidence into worker-run metadata when the orchestration layer records a real worker attempt.
handoff_notes: Stage 7D adds worktree_state.py for read-only branch/base/head/dirty/diff evidence capture. Future cleanup or live git worktree commands should consume these snapshots but remain behind explicit containment guards.
